status: Fixed in backlog-ID maintenance route lane
finding: A prompt that names an existing backlog item such as `ATM-BUG-2026-07-07-048` is interpreted as an ATM ledger task id, so `next --prompt` returns `ATM_NEXT_TASK_SCOPE_NOT_FOUND`; falling back to `start` then routes the repair as `create-atom` and asks for unrelated atom evidence. This interrupts a valid bug-closeout workflow and makes the operator recover manually before implementation can begin.
expectedBehavior: Existing ATM backlog IDs should resolve to their canonical backlog entry and a governed maintenance/closeout route, or `next` should emit one explicit command to open a linked task card. It should not classify the backlog ID as a missing ledger task or route an existing bug fix into atom birth.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed on 2026-07-11 by excluding canonical `ATM-BUG-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN` and `PROJECT-BUG-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN` identifiers from ledger task-id extraction. `npm run validate:prompt-scoped-next` verifies that `Resolve ATM-BUG-2026-07-07-048 and push the fix` routes to `ATM_NEXT_FRAMEWORK_TEMP_CLAIM_REQUIRED`, with no `ATM_NEXT_TASK_SCOPE_NOT_FOUND` and `taskScopeMentioned: false`.
followUp: Keep the backlog-ID routing regression beside prompt-scoped next coverage; a future linked task-card workflow may add richer backlog metadata, but maintenance routing must remain unblocked.
